mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above prior 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons per month signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS=158,286 thousand indicates strong employment
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth rates (Spearman rho < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, which reduces GDP growth expectations and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST=1,321 thousand is near threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or housing declines fail to precede equity weakness by 20-40 trading days in > 50% of test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, signaling simultaneous supply weakness and labor market softening that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO=102.66 and UNRATE=3.9% near trigger threshold
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with supply constraints easing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward returns on both proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month, indicating strong consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS=705,180 million indicates solid consumer activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE=3.9% is historically low but vulnerable to shocks
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or unemployment increases fail to precede equity weakness by 20-60 trading days in > 45% of test periods.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, the Fed faces a policy dilemma that creates uncertainty about future rate cuts, suppressing equity valuations through elevated discount rates and recession risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1=22,768.9 billion and FEDFUNDS=5.33% indicate policy tightness amid slowing growth
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with growth deceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) maintains consistent month-over-month growth above 0.3% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) stabilizes with month-over-month changes below 0.3%, signaling healthy supply-side productivity gains without demand-pull inflation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.3% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO=102.66 and CPIAUCSL=313.21 suggest supply-side stability
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with disinflation and productivity gains
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term fails to improve model fit (R² increase < 0.02) relative to single-proxy models.
